thinking.text:
I found a key document: "Platinum Rewards Card: New Customer Promo - First Year Annual Fee Waived" (doc_credit_cards_platinum_rewards_card_009).

This says:
- Promo window: Start date 2024-06-01, End date 2024-12-31
- If approved during this window, first year annual fee is $0.00 instead of $200.00

But the account was opened on 11/10/2022. That's before the promo window of 2024-06-01 to 2024-12-31. So the first year fee waiver promo wouldn't apply to this account since it was opened before that window.

So Year 1 (11/10/2022 - 11/09/2023) would have had the $200 annual fee billed, and the rebate eligibility would apply to that year.

Wait, but actually the policy says: "If your first cardmember year’s annual fee is waived under a promotional offer, the rebate does not apply to that year. Eligibility begins with the next cardmember year."

Since the account was opened on 11/10/2022 (before the promo window started), there wouldn't have been a promo waiver for the first year. So the rebate would have applied starting from Year 1.

Let me reconsider. Year 1: 11/10/2022 - 11/09/2023 - the rebate eligibility for the $150 annual fee rebate would apply to this year.

But the user said they've "had the card for about a year." Looking at the account open date of 11/10/2022, that's about 3 years ago. So the user might be confused about timing, or perhaps they're referring to something else.

Let me just look at the data and explain what I can see. Let me focus on the most recently completed cardmember year which is Year 3 (11/10/2024 - 11/09/2025).

Actually, let me reconsider. The cardmember year for the rebate is about the $200 annual fee. When was the annual fee billed? If Year 1 had the fee, then the rebate would be assessed after Year 1 (when the annual fee was billed for the next year, or after the 12 monthly windows).

Actually, re-reading the policy:
- "If you qualified, the rebate is applied as a statement credit to your Platinum Rewards Card account after the annual fee for that cardmember year is billed."

So the annual fee is billed at the start of each cardmember year, and the rebate is assessed at the end of the year. So:
- Year 1 (11/10/2022 - 11/09/2023): The $200 annual fee would have been billed at the start (or on first statement). After the 12 monthly windows of Year 1 close (around 11/09/2023), if you met the $7,500 monthly threshold in all months, you'd get a $150 rebate.

Similarly for Year 2 and Year 3.

Let me check the transactions available. I have transaction data from 11/10/2024 to 10/26/2025, which covers Year 3 (11/10/2024 - 11/09/2025).

Year 3 just ended on 11/09/2025 (a few days ago on Nov 9, and today is Nov 14). So the rebate for Year 3 would be evaluated after 11/09/2025.

Based on my calculations, in every month of Year 3 (11/10/2024 - 11/09/2025), the spending was above $7,500, so Yuki appears to qualify for the $150 rebate for Year 3!

Let me just double-check Month 1: 11/10/2024 to 12/09/2024.

Transactions posting in this window:
- 11/10/2024: $424.45 (posts on 11/10 - in window)
- 11/12/2024: $2,126.65 (posts on 11/12 - in window)
- 11/20/2024: $504.92 (posts on 11/20 - in window)
- 11/24/2024: $4,179.24 (posts on 11/24 - in window)
- 12/03/2024: $358.92 (posts on 12/03 - in window)

Total: $424.45 + $2,126.65 + $504.92 + $4,179.24 + $358.92 = $7,594.18 ✓ Above $7,500

All good! Let me present this information to Yuki.

worker_attempts.visible_answer:
Great news, Yuki! I've verified your identity and looked into your account. Let me walk you through what I found.

---

**Your Platinum Rewards Card** was opened on **November 10, 2022**. Here's how the annual fee rebate works:

- **Rebate amount:** **$150** back on your $200 annual fee
- **Monthly threshold:** You need to spend **$7,500** in purchases every month (based on monthly windows from the 10th of one month to the 9th of the next)
- You must hit that threshold in **all 12 months** of a cardmember year to qualify

Looking at your transactions for the most recently completed cardmember year (**Year 3: 11/10/2024 – 11/09/2025**), I calculated your spending in each monthly window, and here's what I found:

| Monthly Window | Total Spending |
|---|---|
| 11/10 - 12/09/2024 | **$7,594.18** ✅ |
| 12/10 - 01/09/2025 | **$7,635.92** ✅ |
| 01/10 - 02/09/2025 | **$7,721.49** ✅ |
| 02/10 - 03/09/2025 | **$7,763.29** ✅ |
| 03/10 - 04/09/2025 | **$7,845.26** ✅ |
| 04/10 - 05/09/2025 | **$7,873.15** ✅ |
| 05/10 - 06/09/2025 | **$7,713.77** ✅ |
| 06/10 - 07/09/2025 | **$7,598.04** ✅ |
| 07/10 - 08/09/2025 | **$7,601.91** ✅ |
| 08/10 - 09/09/2025 | **$7,617.16** ✅ |
| 09/10 - 10/09/2025 | **$7,917.61** ✅ |
| 10/10 - 11/09/2025 | **$7,672.23** ✅ |

**Great news!** 🎉 You met the $7,500 monthly threshold in **every single month** of Year 3. Since that year just closed on November 9th, you should qualify for the **$150 annual fee rebate**! Per policy, the rebate is applied as a statement credit to your account after the evaluation is completed and the annual fee for that year has been billed.

Is there anything else I can help you with?
